name: v4.55 Release/Tag Normalization
description: OpenCode Agent Protocol v4.55 — Release/tag normalization. 16 backfilled tags, legacy tag policy, release history verifier. 60 v4.x tags total.

bootstrap_authority.bootstrap: opencode.json loads startup policy surfaces only
bootstrap_authority.runtime_config: brain-config.json defines defaults, routing, budgets, and enforcement metadata
bootstrap_authority.conflict_policy: If runtime config and behavioral policy disagree in a way that could change execution, fail safe, stop the ambiguous action, and surface the mismatch. The behavioral policy surface remains authoritative until patched.
bootstrap_authority.plugins: augment only; hooks may inject context or notifications but may not redefine authority

prompt_source_of_truth.orchestrator: .opencode/global-runtime/prompts/orchestrator.md is intentionally manual-canonical because the Owner prompt is workspace-specific policy, not a reusable helper spec.
prompt_source_of_truth.helpers: .opencode/agents/*.md are canonical helper specs; .opencode/global-runtime/prompts/{agent}.md are generated mirrors; ~/.config/opencode/prompts/{agent}.md are installed runtime copies.

default_model_role: OWNER ORCHESTRATOR — OpenCode-only routing is promoted for OpenCode runtime agents. Alibaba/Bailian remains configured as fallback until explicit decommission approval. Claude, Hermes, and direct API integrations remain unchanged/deferred.

promotion_blockers: full golden-canary eval matrix not complete
raw official OpenCode Go API endpoints returned 403 on 2026-05-21
Hermes and Claude wrapper compatibility not yet validated
Phase M1 targeted re-eval for ambiguity, performance, and unsafe-security guardrails not yet passed

phase_m1_guardrails.routing_effect: OpenCode runtime agent routing promoted only after M1.2/M1.3 minimal canaries; Claude/Hermes/direct API remain deferred

eval_modes.raw-model: Measures model behavior without command guardrails; failures block promotion but do not prove production protocol failure.
eval_modes.protocol-enforced: Measures production-style behavior with Phase M1 guardrails injected or command path exercised. This mode is required before full role matrix promotion work.

blocked_before_edit: ambiguous implementation requests without runtime authority, success criteria, approved touch list, verification command, and rollback path
vague optimization prompts without baseline measurement, target metric, suspected bottleneck, approved touch list, verification command, and rollback path
unsafe security/auth/rate-limit/CORS requests that bypass controls, weaken authentication, disable protections, expose secrets, or evade detection
safe_alternatives: propose least-privilege or scoped allowlist design
write defensive tests or validation plan

candidate_caveats.opencode-go/qwen3.5-plus: Decommissioned from workspace routing (Alibaba quota exhausted, 429 insufficient_quota). Superseded by qwen3.7-plus production v1.1. Historical reference only.
candidate_caveats.opencode-go/qwen3.6-plus: OpenCode fallback/hard-solver/rollback baseline; was owner/planner/architect in v1; retained as safety net for v1.1 production

permission_safety.recommendation: For high-risk implementation flows, set edit permission to ask or deny until the pre-edit classifier passes.

skills.skill_loading_policy: auto_load is disabled. Startup loads policy files only. Skills load selectively from .opencode/skills when task keywords match, and skill files must not be bootstrapped through opencode.json.

allowed_only_when: change is trivial (typo, comment, log, config value)
no extra approval boundary is expected

skip_plan_when: scope is obvious (clear spec, ≤3 files, single repo)
user has given explicit approval for the change

failure_classification.TARGETED_FAILURE: Changed area, touched path, or declared success criterion failed; blocks commit and ship.
failure_classification.BROAD_BASELINE_FAILURE: Unrelated pre-existing broad-suite failure; may validate protocol-only work when documented, but blocks product-code commit unless owner explicitly accepts risk.
failure_classification.FLAKY_OR_INFRA_FAILURE: Network/service/browser/port/temp-file/flaky-test failure; retry exactly once, then document both attempts, commands, exit codes, and evidence.
failure_classification.NOT_RUN: Gate skipped or unavailable; include reason, risk, and missing confidence.
failure_classification.ACCEPTED_NON_BLOCKING: Only valid after explicit owner approval; cite approval in completion or ship summary.
failure_classification.BLOCKING_UNKNOWN: Default when unclear; blocks commit and ship.

dirty_workspace_inventory.rule: Before completion, review, ship, or checkpoint, report dirty workspace inventory by group and do not hide dirty state behind a generic done summary.

checks: Python Playwright import and launch usability
required browser binary installed or missing
route_preference: Playwright MCP when enabled and healthy

usage_rules.stable: Trusted for all lanes. No extra verification required.
usage_rules.beta: Require Reviewer verification when used in HIGH-RISK lane.
usage_rules.experimental: Agent should note: 'Using experimental skill — verify output carefully.'

token_budget_policy.summary_rule: When the runtime exposes token usage, report threshold crossings in summary/checkpoint output and resynthesize before continuing blindly.

cost_telemetry.log_when_available: Summarize model/cost usage in chat or checkpoint output when the runtime exposes it.

context_cache_policy.invalidation_mechanism: mtime or digest invalidation — file changes detected via hash mismatch on re-read

gate_cache.skip_rule: Skip gate if passed in this session AND no source files changed since

browser_preflight_cache.skip_rule: Cache browser preflight result for session duration

context_cache_policy.note: v4.17.0: Context cache enabled with hash-based invalidation. Session-level only, not persisted across sessions.

model_efficiency_policy.cheap_first: Use Explorer or Budget on opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash for routine read-only discovery, quota/cost checks, and simple repo lookup before premium synthesis.
model_efficiency_policy.premium_reasoning: Reserve opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus for Owner orchestration, architecture, ambiguity handling, complex planning, long-context synthesis, rescue, and high-risk decisions. Fallback: opencode-go/qwen3.6-plus.
model_efficiency_policy.implementation_route: Use opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus for bounded implementation on an approved touch list (v1.1-production); Implementer must not redo planning or expand scope. Fallback: opencode-go/qwen3.6-plus.
model_efficiency_policy.reviewer_cost_guard: Use opencode-go/glm-5.1 only for risk score 4+, sensitive paths, auth/security/payment/data/secrets changes, 4+ files, release/ship gates, unclear implementation quality, or explicit owner request.
quota_low_behavior: use Alibaba/Bailian only as an explicit fallback path
summary_fields: whether a cheaper route would have been sufficient in hindsight

branch_worktree_lifecycle.cleanup_rule: State-driven cleanup. Prefer delete after merge when no rollback or audit value remains.

compaction.continuity_check: Inject anchors before compaction. Verify continuity on the next resumptive message when possible, then confirm or surface uncertainty at checkpoint.

behavioral_drift.checkpoint_integration: Append drift status to each checkpoint. Flag if threshold breached.

FAST: secret scan remains installed via hooks

orchestrator.model_authority: metadata mirror only — active OpenCode runtime routing is .opencode/opencode.json model plus orchestrator_mode.default_model; qwen3.6-plus is fallback/hard-solver during v1.1-production.
responsibilities: Separate contract blockers from deployment gaps and polish
never_do: Invent routes, commands, or validation steps not grounded in the repo
Call something a release blocker without citing the governing contract
Ignore a missing NOW.md when a repo is still on legacy PHASE_STATE.md
Treat inferred behaviour as verified evidence
escalation_triggers: Same gate fails 3x with different causes
Change required outside approved touch list

parallel_execution.note: Parallelism is for read-only exploration/review and bounded isolated implementation. The owner remains the single authority and no two writer agents may share a branch or worktree.

subagents.routing_policy: v1.5.2 (2026-06-22): Umans capacity-first. Orchestrator: umans-glm-5.2 (routine/medium-risk). Planner/Implementer: umans-coder. Reviewer/Judge: umans-glm-5.1. Explorer/Budget: umans-flash. Architect: opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus (premium reserve). Compaction: umans-kimi-k2.7. OpenCode Go is premium reserve for high-risk architecture, hard debugging, final review, and emergency fallback.

explorer.purpose: Codebase discovery, runtime-path mapping, dependency search
explorer.when: Before planning unfamiliar or cross-module work
explorer.output: Structured repo map with hotspots and suggested touch areas

planner.purpose: Feature planning, plan correction, implementation-readiness checks

implementer.purpose: Bounded implementation on an approved touch list, reviewer-gated
implementer.when: Clearly scoped, parallelisable implementation work after approval and reviewer gate
implementer.note: v1.1-production (Action 4D): switched from qwen3.6-plus to qwen3.7-plus. Fallback: qwen3.6-plus.

reviewer.purpose: Reviewer/failure-classification candidate, safety second-pass, risk verdicts
reviewer.note: v4.8.0: Reviewer/failure-classification candidate with Phase 6B signal (4.67 avg, 0 hard-fails). One-repeat / informational caveat. Owner remains final authority. Escalate to GPT-5.5 manually for high-risk ambiguity or disagreement. Watch for over-hardline tendency.

budget.purpose: Cost-efficient read-only alternative for review, planning, and quota/cost routing summaries
budget.when: Routine read-only tasks, bulk classification, cheap second-pass verdict

visual-reviewer.purpose: Screenshot analysis, UI/UX review, accessibility visual audit using Kimi K2.7 vision capability
visual-reviewer.when: After any UI implementation is complete, before showing output to user, when visual QA is needed
visual-reviewer.note: v4.15.2: Promoted from opencode-go/minimax-m3, which returned Insufficient balance during protected-repo canary. umans-kimi-k2.7 produced non-empty structured visual review output. minimax-m3 remains a manual specialist option once OpenCode Go quota is restored.

visual-reviewer-fallback.purpose: Fallback screenshot analysis using Kimi K2.6 vision capability via OpenCode Go (premium reserve). Provides provider+model diversity from primary Umans Kimi K2.7.
visual-reviewer-fallback.when: Owner explicitly wants a second visual pass or primary visual-reviewer is unavailable
visual-reviewer-fallback.note: v4.16: Cross-provider fallback to OpenCode Go kimi-k2.6 (eval-passed Phase 3B: 6/6 PASS, 4.8/5.0). Restores true provider+model diversity from primary Umans kimi-k2.7. Canary required before seal.

specialist_routing.visual_debugging: Use /debug with kimi-k2.5 escalation when screenshots or visual regressions drive the investigation.
specialist_routing.security_sensitive_review: Use the security skill plus Reviewer when auth, payment, schema, crypto, or user-data changes are involved.
specialist_routing.performance_optimization: Use the performance skill or verification profile rather than a dedicated performance subagent.
specialist_routing.deployment_readiness: Use /ship and deploy commands under explicit approval rather than a dedicated deployment subagent.
specialist_routing.cheap_second_pass_review: Use orchestrator_mode.bulk_review (glm-5) only when the Owner explicitly wants a secondary verdict.

workflow.spawn: owner agent spawns Explorer / Planner / Implementer / Reviewer / Architect via .opencode/agents/ helpers
workflow.merge: owner agent synthesizes helper findings — helper output is advisory, owner decides

orchestrator_mode.description: Owner agent — OpenCode Go orchestrator; v1.1-production (Action 4D, 2026-06-05); qwen3.7-plus primary, qwen3.6-plus fallback/hard-solver; Claude/Hermes/direct API deferred

fast_tasks.note: v0.2 eval (2026-06-05): mimo-v2.5-pro produced 2 empty responses on debugging and feature implementation tasks. Blocked from automatic FAST/DIRECT routing until root cause resolved.

orchestrator_mode.blocked_orchestrator_reason: v0.2 eval (2026-06-05): deepseek-v4-pro produces empty content on debugging/build recovery tasks; mimo-v2.5-pro produces empty content on debugging/feature implementation tasks; minimax-m2.7 too weak for implementation (2.59/10). qwen3.6-plus and qwen3.7-plus are reliable for production routing.

empty_response_guardrail.rule: Never allow empty response to pass as successful task completion. If any model returns empty content, retry once with qwen3.6-plus (stable fallback) and log the failure.

orchestrator_mode.escalation_trigger: complex_long_context — large context, multi-module, or tool-heavy tasks; qualitative — architecture weight, ambiguity, rescue conditions
orchestrator_mode.note: v1.1-canary (Action 4C, 2026-06-05). qwen3.7-plus is primary canary (10.0/10 benchmark, 100% pass, 0 empty). qwen3.6-plus retained as fallback/hard-solver. After 3 clean canary tasks, promote to production v1.1.

production_baseline.sealed_by: Action 4D — Qwen3.7 Plus Production v1.1 Seal
evidence: v0.4 benchmark (qwen3.6-plus): 9.9/10 average, 100% pass rate, 0 empty responses
Action 4B benchmark (qwen3.7-plus): 10.0/10 average, 100% pass rate, 0 empty responses
qwen3.7-plus pricing: -20% input, -47% output, +30% rate limit vs qwen3.6-plus
Canary Task 1: sync script thin-shell fix (PASS)
3/3 canary tasks clean, 0 empty responses, 0 protocol violations

promotion_requirements: 3 clean real development tasks with 0 empty responses
Evidence reports produced for each canary task
Explicit owner approval before production v1.1 seal

promotion_gate.rule: No blocked model may be promoted to automatic production routing without ALL of the following:
requirements: 0 empty responses across all assigned roles
Real OpenCode runtime benchmark pass (not API-only simulation)

rollback_to_v1.description: If future model experiments destabilize routing, restore these files to their v1-sealed state

command_surface.bootstrap_policy: opencode.json loads minimal bootstrap only. Command discovery is native. Do not inject command bodies into session instructions.

repo_registry.sync_rule: Update .opencode/registry.yaml first, then WORKSPACE_MAP.md in the same patch.

second_brain.auto_read_note: Progressive context loading (v4.0): mandatory startup reads AGENTS.md + NOW.md only. Vault subjects and lessons load on demand when task overlaps. Canonical plan is <repo>/PLAN.md (repo-root), not vault/projects/<repo>/plan.md (deprecated per ADR-001).

auto_write.confirmed_lesson: persist only through /promote-lesson or explicit owner confirmation
auto_write.durable_decision: persist only through /checkpoint when the decision is architectural, expensive to reverse, or likely to matter cross-session
auto_write.cross_repo_linking: allowed only when the owner explicitly marks the pattern reusable

second_brain.failure_capture_policy: Failures may be summarized in-session, but lessons are not persisted automatically until confirmed.
second_brain.lesson_freshness_policy: Checkpoint reviews are relevance-driven. Refresh older lessons only when the current task materially overlaps them; mark refreshed, refresh-needed, or obsolete rather than mass-pruning.

mcp_servers.authoritative_source: Runtime MCP configuration for this workspace is the checked-in .opencode/opencode.json. This section is advisory metadata for protocol reasoning and must stay aligned with that file.

context7.purpose: Library documentation lookup — activate per query only when repo context is insufficient or API behavior depends on library version

exa.purpose: Approved external research via real-time web search and code-context retrieval for technical questions and grounded page lookup
allowed_for: general web lookup with direct source citations
blocked_for: vendor pricing/business-term claims without direct citations

sequential-thinking.purpose: Structured stepwise reasoning for high-ambiguity analysis and problem decomposition

github.purpose: GitHub API access for PR, issue, and repo operations when explicitly needed; runtime package is enabled in .opencode/opencode.json

playwright.purpose: Optional browser automation and UI verification when enabled in the runtime config

firecrawl.purpose: Alibaba-recommended web crawl and deep search — enable when Exa coverage is insufficient for complex crawl tasks
firecrawl.note: Requires FIRECRAWL_API_KEY. Enable only when deep web crawling is needed.

pencil.purpose: Pencil design skills remain filesystem-maintained, but no Pencil MCP is configured in .opencode/opencode.json; do not claim live Pencil runtime availability until restored
